text: Some think this verse has crept in here by mistake; see 21.1, where a similar historical notice follows the description of Aaron’s death.

‘Eyesores to you, barbs in your side’; the Hebrew text here has ‘spikes in your eyes, thorns in your sides’; the Latin translates the former word by ‘nails’ (or possibly ‘warts’), the latter by ‘spears’. The sense is evidently ‘they will be a permanent irritation to you’.
